metadata.name: Volunteering and Community Service
metadata.description: Regular volunteering is associated with reduced mortality through social connections, sense of purpose, physical activity, and psychological well-being.

sources.citation: Jenkinson CE, Dickens AP, Jones K, et al. Is volunteering a public health intervention? A systematic review and meta-analysis of the health and survival of volunteers. BMC Public Health. 2013;13:773.

Anderson ND, Damianakis T, Kröger E, et al. The benefits associated with volunteering among seniors: a critical review and recommendations for future research. Psychological Bulletin. 2014;140(6):1505-1533.

metadata.mechanismOfAction: Enhanced social connections, sense of purpose and meaning, increased physical activity, improved mental health, reduced depression and anxiety

applicability.notes: Meta-analysis of 40 studies. Benefits strongest in older adults (65+) but observed across age groups.
